core_concept: Properties of Matter: States, Heating, Cooling, Building
facts: Water can exist as a solid (ice), a liquid (water), or a gas (water vapor); when ice melts or water freezes, it is still the same substance — water — just in a different state.
When liquid water evaporates, it becomes water vapor, an invisible gas that mixes with the air; the water does not disappear, it changes state.
Solids like a wooden block keep their shape because their particles are packed tightly; liquids like water take the shape of their container because their particles can slide past each other.
You can describe a rock by its properties: it is hard, rough, and grey; different materials have different properties that help decide what to build with them.
misconceptions: When water evaporates, it turns into nothing and disappears forever.
Ice and water are different substances because ice is cold and solid, while water is liquid.
